action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 3 got 80.19% with CLIP+cosine classifier+incremental training. Early tasks (0-3) suffer more (72-78%) than later tasks (8-9: 82-89%). Analysis: The 2x weighting for current task was biasing training, and incremental training causes drift in old class weights even with full replay. Since the backbone is frozen, we can accumulate ALL features and train the classifier jointly at the end - this eliminates catastrophic forgetting entirely. Decision: Switch to joint training (extract features sequentially, train classifier on all 45K samples at once) with class-balanced sampling, 300 epochs, and prototype initialization.
